KO_H01: 사회에는 사람들 사이의 이해관계를 공정하게 조율해주는 상위 권력, 예를 들어 국가나 법 같은 것이 있을 수 있다. 그런 권력이 제대로 작동하면 사람들은 규칙에 따라 서로의 권리를 지켜주게 된다. 그런데 만약 그런 상위 권력이 공정한 질서를 만들어주지 않는 상황, 즉 누가 강제하거나 벌을 주지 않는 상황이라면, 사람들이 스스로 알아서 다른 사람의 권리를 침해하지 않고 존중하기란 매우 어렵다. 왜냐하면 인간은 외부의 강제 없이 자발적으로 남의 몫을 인정하기보다는 자기 이익을 먼저 챙기려는 경향이 있기 때문이다.
KO_H02: 혁명이란 기존의 지배 체제를 무너뜨리고 새로운 체제를 세우려는 시도이다. 그런데 혁명에는 큰 위험이 따른다. 혁명을 일으킨 사람들이 싸워 이기더라도, 그 과정에서 원래 자기들의 적이었던 세력이 오히려 새로운 권력을 잡게 되는 경우가 역사적으로 매우 흔하다. 따라서 혁명의 결과로 원래의 적이 새 지배자가 되지 않는 것만으로도, 즉 최악의 결과만 피한 것만으로도 운이 좋은 편이라고 할 수 있을 만큼, 혁명이 원래 의도한 대로 성공하기는 극히 어렵다.
KO_H03: 자유란 내가 원하는 것을 할 수 있는 상태를 말한다. 그런데 자유롭다는 것은 동시에, 내가 한 선택과 행동의 결과를 온전히 내가 감당해야 한다는 뜻이기도 하다. 누군가가 대신 결정해주면 잘못되었을 때 그 사람 탓을 할 수 있지만, 자유로운 상태에서는 모든 결과가 나의 책임이 된다. 바로 이 무거운 책임 때문에 대부분의 사람들은 진정한 자유를 원한다고 말하면서도 실제로는 그것을 두려워하고 회피한다.
KO_H04: 인간이 겪는 모든 종류의 불행에는 하나의 공통된 원인이 있다. 그것은 인간이 자기 방, 즉 자기만의 고요한 공간에서 아무것도 하지 않고 가만히 머무는 것을 견디지 못한다는 점이다. 사람은 혼자 조용히 있으면 지루함이나 불안을 느끼기 때문에, 끊임없이 바깥으로 나가 무언가를 하려 하고 자극을 찾아 돌아다닌다. 그렇게 가만있지 못하고 밖으로 나서는 과정에서 갈등, 위험, 실패 등 온갖 불행을 만나게 된다는 뜻이다.
KO_H05: 사람이 할 수 있는 여행 중에서 가장 멀고 어려운 여행은 물리적으로 먼 곳을 가는 것이 아니라, 자기 자신의 내면, 즉 자신의 진짜 감정, 욕망, 두려움, 본질을 깊이 들여다보는 과정이다. 그리고 그렇게 자기 내면을 충분히 탐색하고 이해한 뒤에, 있는 그대로의 자기 자신을 받아들이고 선택한 사람만이, 비로소 다른 사람과 진정한 관계를 맺고 다가갈 수 있는 자격을 갖게 된다. 자기 자신을 모르는 사람은 타인도 제대로 이해하거나 대할 수 없다는 뜻이다.
KO_L01: 오늘 아침에 보통 때보다 이른 시각에 잠에서 깨어 일어났다. 그런 다음 따뜻하게 데운 물을 사용하여 얼굴을 씻었다. 세수를 마친 뒤에는 아침 식사로 밥을 먹었다. 식사를 마친 후 학교로 이동하여 그곳에서 낮 동안 시간을 보냈다. 학교 일과가 끝난 저녁 시간대에 학교를 떠나 자기 집으로 돌아왔다.
KO_L02: 어제, 그 사람은 서울이라는 도시에서 출발하여 부산이라는 도시까지 이동했다. 이동 수단으로는 기차를 이용했다. 부산에서 일정을 마친 뒤, 오늘 다시 같은 경로를 되돌아와서 원래 있던 서울로 돌아왔다.
KO_L03: 어제, 나는 미리 약속한 친구를 만났다. 만난 뒤에 둘이 함께 카페라는 음료를 파는 가게에 갔다. 카페에서 커피를 각자 주문하여 마셨고, 커피를 마시면서 서로 여러 가지 이야기를 주고받으며 대화를 나눴다.
KO_L04: 주말, 즉 토요일이나 일요일에 가족 구성원들과 다 같이 집에서 가까운 거리에 있는 공원에 갔다. 공원에 도착한 뒤에는 함께 걸으며 산책을 했다. 산책을 하다가 공원에 있는 벤치, 즉 앉을 수 있는 긴 의자에 앉았다. 거기에 앉아서 미리 집에서 싸온 도시락을 꺼내 함께 먹었다.
KO_L05: 아침 시간에 지하철이라는 지하로 다니는 전동차를 타고 자신이 일하는 회사까지 출근했다. 낮 12시쯤 점심시간이 되자 회사 근처에 있는 식당에 가서 점심 식사로 밥을 먹었다. 점심을 먹은 뒤 오후 시간대에 다시 회사로 돌아가서 남은 업무를 계속했다.
EN_H01: As a nation, we are currently in a time of serious crisis and many people feel afraid of the economic hardship and uncertain future we face. But I am telling you that the real danger is not the crisis itself — the real danger is the emotion of fear that we feel in response to it. This kind of fear has no specific identifiable cause, does not respond to logical thinking, and has no basis in reality proportional to how strongly it is felt. This irrational terror stops people from taking the necessary actions that would allow us to stop retreating from our problems and instead begin making forward progress toward solving them. Therefore, if we can overcome our own fear, we can overcome everything else.
EN_H02: If a person spends a long time fighting against evil people or cruel systems, that person must be careful to pay attention to their own behavior and character, because there is a real risk that in the process of fighting evil, the person will gradually adopt the same cruel and immoral methods and mindset as the evil they are fighting, thereby becoming an evil person themselves. This is illustrated by a metaphor: if you stare for a long time into a deep, dark, empty void — which represents darkness, evil, or meaninglessness — that experience changes you in return, as though the darkness is studying you back and seeping into you. The process of deeply engaging with darkness, even to oppose it, transforms the person who does so.
EN_H03: When a person gains political or social power — meaning the ability to control or influence other people and events — that power has a natural tendency to make the person morally worse, meaning more selfish, dishonest, or willing to harm others for personal benefit. Furthermore, when a person gains complete and unchecked power with no limits or accountability, that person will inevitably become completely morally corrupt without exception. Because of this pattern, people who are considered historically great or important are, in almost every case, also morally bad people. This is true even when those people influence others only through persuasion, reputation, or ideas rather than through formal authority such as holding an official position that lets them command or punish others. Even soft, informal influence over others is enough to produce moral corruption.
EN_H04: If you think of the overall moral progress of human civilization as a physical path or arc stretching across a very long period of time, that path does gradually curve in the direction of justice — meaning that over very long time spans, societies do tend to become more fair, more equal, and more respectful of human rights. However, this progress is extremely slow and is not at all automatic or guaranteed. The second part of the statement adds a crucial condition: this bending toward justice only happens when a sufficient number of people actively work and struggle to make it happen. The metaphor of hands reaching up to pull the arc down means that ordinary people must exert deliberate effort, protest, sacrifice, and collective action to force moral progress to occur. Without that active human effort, the arc will not bend on its own.
EN_H05: When a person is in the middle of a difficult, challenging, or painful situation, there is hidden within that difficulty a chance to improve, grow, learn, or create something better — an opportunity that would not exist without the difficulty. However, most people do not automatically see this opportunity because finding it requires a specific kind of bravery: the willingness to stop doing what you have been doing even though it is familiar and comfortable, specifically when what you have been doing has already proven not to work. People naturally cling to failed approaches because the failure is at least predictable and known, whereas trying something new is uncertain and frightening. So the real challenge is not just that the situation is hard, but that seeing the way forward requires you to let go of your attachment to a failed but familiar course of action.
EN_L01: On the morning of the day I am telling you about, which is today, I stopped sleeping and became conscious at a time that was earlier than the time I usually wake up. After getting out of bed, I went to a bathroom that contains a sink, and I used a toothbrush and toothpaste to clean my teeth. After that, I put on clothing appropriate for the day — replacing whatever I had been sleeping in — choosing items from clothing I own that are kept in my home. Then I went to the kitchen, which is a room in my home dedicated to preparing and sometimes eating food, and I sat down at a table located in that kitchen. There I ate a meal called breakfast, which is the first meal people typically eat after waking up in the morning. After finishing that meal, I left my home, got into a car that I own or regularly use, started the engine, and operated the car to drive along roads from my home to the location where I am employed and do my job.
EN_L02: A woman or girl that the speaker knows walked on foot — meaning she traveled using her own legs along sidewalks or streets — to a retail store that sells food and other goods, which is located at a street corner near where she was. At that store, she selected a sealed container of drinkable water and a sandwich, which is a food item made of bread with fillings such as meat, cheese, or vegetables between the slices, and she paid money to the store in exchange for these items. After leaving the store, she went to a park, which is a public outdoor green space in her area, and she found a bench, which is a long seat typically made of wood or metal that is placed outdoors for people to sit on. She sat down on that bench and ate the water and sandwich she had just purchased as her midday meal, which is called lunch.
EN_L03: On the day before today, during the afternoon portion of the day, a male person who shares at least one parent with the speaker — his brother — traveled from wherever the brother lives to the house where the speaker lives, arriving there for a casual social visit. Once the brother was there, the two of them sat together on a couch, which is a large soft piece of furniture designed for multiple people to sit on, located in the speaker's home, and they watched a movie, which is a long video story, on a television or screen in front of the couch. When it came time for dinner, which is the main evening meal, instead of cooking food themselves, they used a phone or app to place an order with a restaurant that makes and delivers pizza, which is a flat round bread topped with cheese, sauce, and other toppings that is baked in an oven. The pizza was delivered to the speaker's house and they ate it. Then, at approximately nine o'clock in the evening, the brother left the speaker's house and traveled back to wherever the brother lives.
EN_L04: On the morning of today, the speaker left their home and traveled to a public library — which is a building run by the local government where anyone can borrow books and other materials for free — by riding a bus, which is a large vehicle that carries many passengers along a fixed route through the city for a small fare. At the library, the speaker returned three books that they had previously borrowed, and these books had reached or were approaching their due date, which is the deadline by which borrowed books must be brought back to avoid late fees or penalties. The speaker then selected two different books that they had not previously borrowed, and went through the library's checkout process to officially borrow those books and take them home for a set period of time. After completing this, the speaker left the library, traveled back home by some means of transportation, and arrived at home before the time of day when people typically eat lunch, which is the midday meal usually eaten around noon.
EN_L05: After the speaker finished their work for the day at the place where they are employed, on their way driving home, they made an additional stop at a grocery store, which is a retail shop that sells food and common household items. Inside the store, the speaker found and purchased milk, which is a white liquid that comes from cows and is used for drinking and cooking, and eggs, which are oval-shelled reproductive products from chickens used widely in cooking. After paying for these items, the speaker got back into their car, drove the remaining distance to the home where they live, and went inside. Once home, the speaker prepared a dinner meal that was not elaborate or complicated, using basic cooking methods and likely some of the ingredients they had at home. After eating that dinner, the speaker turned on a television set — which is an electronic screen that receives broadcast signals — and watched a news program, which is a regularly scheduled broadcast where journalists report on recent important events happening locally and around the world.
